varieties.name: Hybrid YD14
varieties.yield_notes: Medium yielding
varieties.availability: Queensland specialist growers

varieties.name: RB1 (Red Bisexual 1)
varieties.yield_notes: Good production
varieties.availability: Papaya Seed Australia, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: RD1 (Red Dioecious 1)
varieties.yield_notes: Good production
varieties.availability: Queensland papaya growers

varieties.name: Sunrise Solo 🏆
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, up to 100 fruits per year

varieties.name: Southern Red Bisexual
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy cropper, prolonged fruiting period, can produce 5+ fruits per week in summer
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Nursery (Queensland), Ross Evans Garden Centre, Ladybird Nursery

varieties.name: Richter Gold
varieties.traits: Yellow dioecious (requires male and female plants)
varieties.yield_notes: Good production
varieties.availability: Queensland specialty nurseries, local growers

varieties.name: Sunnybank 🏆
varieties.yield_notes: Good yield for subtropical conditions

varieties.name: Bettina 🏆
varieties.origin: Queensland, Australia (cross between Florida Betty × Queensland strain)
varieties.yield_notes: Good production
varieties.availability: Queensland heritage plant nurseries

varieties.name: Guinea Gold
varieties.yield_notes: Good for subtropical conditions, 15-18 months to maturity

varieties.name: Kapoho Solo 🏆
varieties.origin: Hawaii, USA (developed 1950s, adapted for Puna district)
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent productivity, consistent fruit shape and size
varieties.availability: International seed suppliers, specialty tropical fruit nurseries

varieties.name: Waimanalo 🏆
varieties.yield_notes: Low-bearing habit (fruits low to ground), first flowers ~6 months from seed, 66% hermaphrodite/33% female ratio
varieties.availability: Hawaiian seed suppliers, specialty importers, some Australian tropical fruit nurseries

varieties.name: Sunset 🏆
varieties.yield_notes: High fruit production
varieties.availability: Hawaiian seed sources, international suppliers

varieties.name: Red Lady
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yield, quick production (flowers 3 months, harvest 4-5 months from planting), semi-dwarf habit (~2m height), fruits at 4 feet
varieties.availability: Widely available at Australian nurseries, garden centers, seed suppliers

varieties.name: Tainung No. 2 (Taiwan Baby)
varieties.traits: Hybrid developed for tropical/subtropical climates, gynodioecious
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yields, extended production period, disease resistant, accounts for 90% of Taiwan production
varieties.availability: International seed suppliers, Asian seed companies, specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Maradol (Caribbean Red) 🏆
varieties.origin: Cuba (long breeding process), now grown Mexico/Caribbean
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, 95% of Mexican production
varieties.availability: Some Australian importers, international seed suppliers

varieties.name: TR Hovey (Pusa Dwarf type)
varieties.yield_notes: ~40kg per plant, fruits at 40cm height, maximum height 106cm
varieties.availability: International seed suppliers, Indian seed companies

varieties.name: Formosa (Tainung)
varieties.origin: Taiwan (also called Farmosa, Brazilian Formosa in some regions)
varieties.yield_notes: High production, trees to 4m height, fruits 5-8 months after sowing
varieties.availability: International suppliers, Brazilian seed companies

varieties.name: Eksotika
varieties.traits: Self-pollinated inbred, similar to Sunrise Solo but larger
varieties.yield_notes: Good bearer, ~60t/ha/year commercial

varieties.name: Red Lady
varieties.origin: Taiwan/International - Most popular hybrid in India
varieties.traits: Gynodioecious hybrid, predominantly used for processing
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yield, short dwarf variety, flowers within 3 months of planting, harvest 4-5 months after planting
varieties.availability: Widely available throughout tropical monsoon climates - Agriearth, tropical monsoon climates nurseries, tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, local suppliers in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Washington
varieties.traits: Dioecious (requires separate male and female plants)
varieties.yield_notes: 60kg per plant average

varieties.name: Coorg Honey Dew (Madhubindu) 🌱
varieties.origin: Karnataka/tropical monsoon climates region, India (indigenous to Western Ghats area)

varieties.name: Red Ruby
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates nurseries, online suppliers like Agriearth

varieties.name: Pusa Delicious
varieties.origin: IARI (Indian Agricultural Research Institute), Pusa, New Delhi
varieties.yield_notes: Begins yielding 8 months after planting, good production
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, Indian agricultural stations, IARI suppliers

varieties.name: Pusa Nanha
varieties.origin: IARI, mutant of Pusa Dwarf, New Delhi
varieties.yield_notes: First fruit at 60-75cm height, plant maximum height 106cm, 10.1kg per plant, tolerant to waterlogging
varieties.availability: IARI, tropical monsoon climates agricultural stations, Indian seed suppliers

varieties.name: Pusa Dwarf
varieties.yield_notes: ~40kg per plant, fruit at 40cm height, suitable for kitchen gardening and high-density cultivation
varieties.availability: IARI, Indian agricultural institutions, tropical monsoon climates nurseries

varieties.name: Pusa Giant
varieties.yield_notes: Strong sturdy growth withstands winds, starts fruiting at minimum 1 meter height
varieties.availability: IARI, agricultural universities, Indian suppliers

varieties.name: Pusa Majesty
varieties.yield_notes: Good production

varieties.name: CO-1
varieties.origin: TNAU, Coimbatore - Selection from Ranchi cultivar
varieties.yield_notes: First fruit 60-75cm height, dwarf habit
varieties.availability: TNAU, tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, Andhra Pradesh nurseries

varieties.name: CO-2 🌱
varieties.origin: TNAU, Coimbatore (local type selection from Tamil Nadu)
varieties.yield_notes: 80-100 fruits per plant, 200-250 t/ha, excellent for papain latex production (~30g latex per fruit, 600kg/ha papain yield)
varieties.availability: TNAU, tropical monsoon climates agricultural stations, Tamil Nadu nurseries

varieties.name: CO-3
varieties.yield_notes: 100-120 t/ha production
varieties.availability: TNAU, South Indian suppliers

varieties.name: CO-5
varieties.origin: TNAU, Coimbatore (developed by crossing CO-2 and Sunrise Solo)
varieties.yield_notes: 90-120 fruits per plant, 200-250 t/ha, excellent for papain (800kg/ha papain yield)

varieties.name: CO-6
varieties.origin: TNAU, Coimbatore (cross between CO-1 and Washington)
varieties.yield_notes: 120-160 t/ha, 80 fruits per plant over 2 years
varieties.availability: TNAU, South Indian agricultural stations

varieties.name: CO-7
varieties.origin: TNAU, Coimbatore (advanced hybrid: Surya × Tainung-1 × Local Dwarf)
varieties.yield_notes: 200-225 t/ha, released variety
varieties.availability: TNAU, Tamil Nadu nurseries

varieties.name: CO-8
varieties.yield_notes: 220-230 t/ha, highest yielding of CO series, excellent for papain (800kg/ha)

varieties.name: Surya (Arka Surya)
varieties.origin: ICAR-IIHR (Indian Institute of Horticultural Research), Bangalore
varieties.yield_notes: Good production recommended for various Indian states

varieties.name: Arka Prabhat

varieties.traits: Gynodioecious, seeds can be produced by bagging hermaphrodite flowers
varieties.yield_notes: Good yield potential
varieties.availability: ICAR-IIHR Bangalore, tropical monsoon climates agricultural sources

varieties.name: Ranchi 🌱
varieties.yield_notes: Single tree can bear many fruits per season

varieties.name: RCTP-1
varieties.yield_notes: Performs well in Goa conditions
varieties.availability: Goa agricultural stations, tropical monsoon climates coastal region nurseries

varieties.name: Native Papaya ROF Type 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu/tropical monsoon climates (Rangamalai Organic Farms community seed bank)
varieties.yield_notes: Traditional productivity

varieties.name: Tainung No. 1
varieties.yield_notes: High yield
varieties.availability: International seed companies, tropical monsoon climates agricultural research stations
